name: read_text_file
description: Read the complete contents of a text file from the file system. Handles common encodings and returns the full body. Use head or tail to slice. Works only within allowed directories.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to read

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

name: read_media_file
description: Read an image or audio file and return its base64-encoded bytes plus a MIME type. Only reads within allowed directories.

path.description: Absolute path to an image or audio file

name: read_multiple_files
description: Read the contents of multiple files in a single call. Each file's content is returned tagged with its path. Failures for individual files do not abort the batch.

paths.description: Array of absolute file paths to read; each must point to an allowed file

name: write_file
description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file. No warning is emitted for overwrites. Text content is written using UTF-8. Only writes within allowed directories.

path.description: Absolute path of the file to create or overwrite

content.description: Full text content to write to the file

name: edit_file
description: Apply line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces an exact oldText segment with newText. Returns a git-style diff of the changes.

path.description: Absolute path to the file being edited

edits.description: Array of { oldText, newText } edits applied in order

dryRun.description: If true, preview changes as a diff without touching the file

name: create_directory
description: Create a directory (or ensure one already exists). Creates nested parent directories in a single call. Silent success if the directory is already present.

path.description: Absolute path of the directory to create

name: list_directory
description: Return a flat listing of a directory's immediate children. File and directory entries are distinguished with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. Read-only.

path.description: Absolute path of the directory to list

name: list_directory_with_sizes
description: Same as list_directory but adds per-entry file sizes and a total summary. Useful for spotting large files before a download.

path.description: Absolute path of the directory to list

sortBy.description: Sort entries alphabetically by name or descending by size

name: directory_tree
description: Return a recursive JSON tree of files and directories under the given root. Each entry has name, type, and children. Respects an optional list of exclude globs.

path.description: Absolute path of the root directory to walk

excludePatterns.description: Glob patterns of entries to skip (e.g. **/node_modules)

name: move_file
description: Move or rename a file or directory. Fails if the destination already exists. Works across directories; both endpoints must be within allowed roots.

source.description: Absolute path of the source file or directory

destination.description: Absolute path of the new location; must not exist

name: search_files
description: Recursively find files and directories whose paths match a glob. Returns full paths of every match. Great when you know the name shape but not the location.

path.description: Absolute directory to begin the search from

excludePatterns.description: Glob patterns to exclude from results

name: get_file_info
description: Return metadata about a file or directory including size, creation time, last modified time, and permissions. Does not read the file body.

path.description: Absolute path whose metadata should be returned

name: list_allowed_directories
description: Return the list of directory roots this server is permitted to access. Subdirectories within those roots are also reachable.

name: watch_path
description: Register a watcher on a path and stream events when files are created, modified, or deleted. Useful for long-running build and test loops.

path.description: Absolute path of the file or directory to watch

recursive.description: If true, watch nested subdirectories as well

name: copy_file
description: Copy a file from one location to another. Overwrites the destination only when explicitly allowed. Both paths must be inside allowed directories.

source.description: Absolute path of the file to copy

destination.description: Absolute path of the copy target

overwrite.description: When true, replaces an existing destination file
